title: Redundant casts should be avoided
description: Casting expressions are utilized to convert one data type to another, such as transforming an integer into a string. This is especially crucial in strongly typed languages like C, C++, C#, Java, Python, and others. However, there are instances where casting expressions are not needed.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Redundant cast detected - variable already has target type
Redundant pointer cast detected - source and target types are identical
Redundant function pointer or array cast detected
Redundant cast identified - remove unnecessary type conversion
